name: create_event
description:
Create an event in Google Calendar, including the user's connected one.

Parameters:
- summary: Event title (required).
- start_time/end_time: Event start and end timestamps (required). Use YYYY-MM-DD for all-day events, otherwise use RFC3339 timestamps with the user's timezone offset from viewer context (e.g. 1970-01-31T12:00:00-08:00). Always include the offset — never send bare datetimes.
- description: Optional event body/notes.
- location: Optional location.
- attendees: Optional list of email addresses to invite. Remember to pass this as an array, even if it contains just one attendee. In almost all cases, you should invite the user.
- recurrence: Optional RFC 5545 recurrence rules. Do not include DTSTART or DTEND.
- add_video_conference: Set to true to attach a Google Meet link.
- timezone: IANA timezone (e.g. "America/Chicago") for the event times. Always provide this.
- send_updates: "all", "externalOnly", or "none" (default). Only set to "all" if the user explicitly asks to send invites or notify attendees, or if you believe notifications are important for the event.
- calendar_id: Optional calendar ID: use to create an event on a specific calendar. A calendar_id is in email format: people's email addresses correspond to their primary calendars. Defaults to the user's primary calendar.

If the user has a request relative to today, like "create an event for tomorrow" or "schedule for next week",
compute the actual dates or timestamps for start_time and end_time rather than using relative words in query.

The result of this tool contains the created event's event_id, which you can use to get, update, delete, and RSVP to this event.

start_time.description: Start time as RFC3339 with timezone offset (e.g. 1970-01-31T12:00:00-08:00), or YYYY-MM-DD for all-day events. Always include the user's timezone offset from viewer context.

end_time.description: End time as RFC3339 with timezone offset (e.g. 1970-01-31T13:00:00-08:00), or YYYY-MM-DD for all-day events. Always include the user's timezone offset from viewer context.

attendees.description: List of attendee email addresses to invite.

recurrence.description: RFC 5545 recurrence rules (e.g. ["RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO,WE,FR"]). Do not include DTSTART or DTEND.

add_video_conference.description: When true, a Google Meet video conference link is added to the event.

timezone.description: IANA timezone for the event (e.g. "America/Los_Angeles"). Always provide this.

send_updates.description: Whether to send email notifications to attendees: "all" (send to all attendees), "externalOnly" (only non-Google attendees), or "none" (no emails). Default to "none" unless the user explicitly asks to notify or invite attendees.

calendar_id.description: Calendar ID to create the event on (e.g. email address from google_calendar_list_calendars). Defaults to the user's primary calendar.

file_url.description: Google Drive file URL (web_view_link from google_drive_search)

attachments.description: Google Drive files to attach to the event. Each entry needs a file_url, which you can get from the web_view_link result of the google_drive_search tool.
